title: Mechanism-Specific Learning Rates
research_question: Are promising architectures being held back by shared broad learning rates rather than by the mechanism itself?

candidates.name: n1-attn-up-mlp-down
candidates.goal: Train attention faster than MLP to see whether the current frontier is attention-underdated.
candidates.hypothesis: The model may now be more attention-limited than MLP-limited, so attention weights need to move faster relative to MLP weights.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether subsystem-specific LR splits outperform one shared matrix LR.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the cleanest first test of 'not all matrix parameters should learn at the same speed'.
candidates.description: raise attention LR and lower MLP LR relative to the matrix baseline

candidates.name: n2-attn-down-mlp-up
candidates.hypothesis: The current frontier may instead be MLP-underdated or over-rotating attention updates.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the direction of subsystem imbalance is real or imagined.
candidates.why_worthy: This brackets the first mechanism-specific LR split instead of assuming one direction.
candidates.description: lower attention LR and raise MLP LR relative to the matrix baseline

candidates.name: n3-late-layer-boost
candidates.goal: Give later layers a modest learning-rate advantage over early layers.
candidates.hypothesis: The top of the stack may be under-updated under a uniform layer LR because it carries more output-facing burden.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the learning-rate imbalance is across depth rather than across module type.
candidates.why_worthy: This is a genuinely different axis from attention-vs-MLP splits.
candidates.description: boost late-layer LR slightly above early-layer LR

candidates.name: n4-early-layer-boost
candidates.goal: Test the mirror image of the depth-wise LR split.
candidates.hypothesis: The bottom of the stack may need more aggressive learning because it builds all later representations.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether early or late layers are the better target for LR asymmetry.
candidates.why_worthy: This completes the depth-wise LR bracket instead of biasing toward one story.
candidates.description: boost early-layer LR slightly above late-layer LR

candidates.name: n5-new-mechanism-boost
candidates.goal: Give newly introduced mechanism weights their own faster LR rather than training them at the same speed as the old backbone.
candidates.hypothesis: Some future architecture ideas fail because their new parameters learn too slowly relative to the pretrained-like backbone of the script.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether architecture failures are partly optimization failures in disguise.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the tranche's bridge into future bold architectures like latent-KV or mixers.
candidates.description: assign a faster LR to new mechanism parameters relative to the backbone
